# EdgeBase Java SDK

Use this file as a quick-reference contract for AI coding assistants working with the
public Java SDK packages in this directory.

## Package Boundary

Use the Java SDK only in trusted JVM-side code.

Do not ship the admin package to Android apps or browser bundles. The client package
is for app and desktop JVM clients, while the admin package is for backend services,
batch jobs, and operational tooling that hold a Service Key.

## Source Of Truth

- Root README: https://github.com/edge-base/edgebase/blob/main/packages/sdk/java/README.md
- Admin README: https://github.com/edge-base/edgebase/blob/main/packages/sdk/java/packages/admin/README.md
- SDK Overview: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks
- Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks/client-vs-server
- Admin Users: https://edgebase.fun/docs/authentication/admin-users
- Database Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/database/admin-sdk
- Push Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/push/admin-sdk
- Analytics Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/analytics/admin-sdk
- Admin SDK Reference: https://edgebase.fun/docs/admin-sdk/reference

## Public Artifacts

- JitPack group: `com.github.edge-base.edgebase`
- tags are versioned with the leading `v`, for example `v0.2.7`
- `com.github.edge-base.edgebase:edgebase-core-java:v0.2.7`
- `com.github.edge-base.edgebase:edgebase-android-java:v0.2.7`
- `com.github.edge-base.edgebase:edgebase-admin-java:v0.2.7`
- do not recommend the root `edgebase-sdk-java` artifact for public installs

## Canonical Examples

### Create a client

```java
import dev.edgebase.sdk.client.ClientEdgeBase;
import dev.edgebase.sdk.client.EdgeBase;

ClientEdgeBase client = EdgeBase.client("https://your-project.edgebase.fun");
```

### Query a table

```java
List<?> posts = client.db("shared")
    .table("posts")
    .getList();
```

### Create an admin client

```java
import dev.edgebase.sdk.admin.AdminEdgeBase;
import dev.edgebase.sdk.client.EdgeBase;

AdminEdgeBase admin = EdgeBase.admin(
    "https://your-project.edgebase.fun",
    System.getenv("EDGEBASE_SERVICE_KEY")
);
```

### Manage users

```java
Map<String, Object> created = admin.adminAuth().createUser(Map.of(
    "email", "june@example.com",
    "password", "pass1234",
    "displayName", "June"
));
```

### Execute SQL

```java
List<Object> sharedRows = admin.sql("shared", "SELECT 1 AS ok");
List<Object> rows = admin.sql(
    "workspace",
    "ws-1",
    "SELECT * FROM documents WHERE status = ?",
    List.of("published")
);
```

## Hard Rules

- keep Service Keys on trusted servers only
- use `dev.edgebase.sdk.client.EdgeBase` for client construction and `dev.edgebase.sdk.admin.AdminEdgeBase` for admin work
- use method accessors such as `admin.adminAuth()` and `admin.storage()`
- `db(namespace, instanceId)` is the canonical instance-id form
- `sql(namespace, query)`, `sql(namespace, query, params)`, `sql(namespace, id, query)`, and `sql(namespace, id, query, params)` are supported overloads
- `broadcast(channel, event, payload?)` is server-side only
- `setContext(...)` and `getContext()` remain available for compatibility
- `dev.edgebase.sdk.core.EdgeBaseFieldOps` provides atomic increment and delete helpers

## Common Mistakes

- do not use `edgebase-admin-java` in client-side Android or browser code
- do not write Kotlin-style property access such as `admin.adminAuth`
- do not use a made-up `createAdminClient(...)` helper in Java
- do not pass an instance id in a named object; use the overloaded `db(...)` and `sql(...)` signatures
- do not expose the Service Key through mobile or browser clients

## Quick Reference

```text
EdgeBase.client(url)                                    -> ClientEdgeBase
EdgeBase.client(url, tokenStorage)                      -> ClientEdgeBase
EdgeBase.admin(url, serviceKey)                         -> AdminEdgeBase
EdgeBase.admin(url, serviceKey, projectId)              -> AdminEdgeBase
client.auth()                                           -> AuthClient
client.db(namespace)                                    -> DbRef
client.db(namespace, instanceId)                        -> DbRef
client.storage()                                        -> StorageClient
client.functions()                                      -> FunctionsClient
client.analytics()                                      -> AnalyticsClient
admin.adminAuth()                                       -> AdminAuthClient
admin.storage()                                         -> StorageClient
admin.functions()                                       -> FunctionsClient
admin.analytics()                                       -> AnalyticsClient
admin.db(namespace)                                     -> DbRef
admin.db(namespace, instanceId)                         -> DbRef
admin.sql(namespace, query)                             -> List<Object>
admin.sql(namespace, query, params)                     -> List<Object>
admin.sql(namespace, id, query)                         -> List<Object>
admin.sql(namespace, id, query, params)                -> List<Object>
admin.broadcast(channel, event, payload?)              -> void
admin.destroy()                                         -> void
EdgeBaseFieldOps.increment(value)                       -> Map<String, Object>
EdgeBaseFieldOps.deleteField()                          -> Map<String, Object>
```
